response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a accomplished web and full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience, but he is fundamentally mismatched with this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, the position mandates native-level Japanese language proficiency, which the candidate entirely lacks — he lists English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond the language disqualifier, his technical background is centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and web platforms rather than the embedded C firmware development, Linux kernel modification, and LAN-switch security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP) the role demands. His exposure to C and embedded systems is minimal and largely historical.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience spanning web, mobile, and limited embedded contexts
Some low-level systems exposure: C/C++ work at National Instruments and a Linux/C++ wave field synthesis thesis project
Strong software craftsmanship foundation (TDD, CI/CD, open-source project ownership) that would transfer to any engineering environment
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard, non-negotiable requirement for the role
Zero experience with the required security networking stack: MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, NETCONF, SNMP, or switch-chip firmware
Primary technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js web development; C and embedded systems work is incidental and underdeveloped relative to role expectations

key_factors.factor: No security networking or embedded firmware experience
Long career with diverse engineering exposure
